```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"fmt"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"net/url"
	"strings"
	"time"

	"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
	"golang.org/x/net/html"
)

// Request body
type PreviewRequest struct {
	URL string `json:"url"`
}

// Response body  
type PreviewResponse struct {
	Title       string `json:"title,omitempty"`
	Description string `json:"description,omitempty"`
}

func FetchURLPreview(ctx echo.Context) error {
	req := new(PreviewRequest)
	if err := ctx.Bind(req); err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "Invalid JSON"})
	}

	// Validate URL
	parsedURL, err := url.Parse(req.URL)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "Invalid URL format"})
	}

	if parsedURL.Scheme != "http" && parsedURL.Scheme != "https" {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "Only HTTP/HTTPS URLs are allowed"})
	}

	// Fetch the URL
	client := &http.Client{Timeout: 3 * time.Second}
	reqHttp, err := http.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, parsedURL.String(), nil)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": "Failed to create request"})
	}
	reqHttp.Header.Set("User-Agent", "Mozilla/5.0 (compatible; URLPreviewBot/1.0)")

	resp, err := client.Do(reqHttp)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": "Failed to fetch URL"})
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()

	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": fmt.Sprintf("HTTP %d", resp.StatusCode)})
	}

	body, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": "Failed to read response body"})
	}

	title, description := extractTitleAndDescription(body)

	return ctx.JSON(http.StatusOK, PreviewResponse{
		Title:       title,
		Description: description,
	})
}

func extractTitleAndDescription(htmlContent []byte) (string, string) {
	doc, err := html.Parse(bytes.NewReader(htmlContent))
	if err != nil {
		return "", ""
	}

	var title, description string
	foundTitle := false
	foundDesc := false

	var findContent func(*html.Node)
	findContent = func(n *html.Node) {
		if foundTitle && foundDesc {
			return
		}

		if n.Type == html.ElementNode {
			if !foundTitle && n.Data == "title" {
				// Get text content from title element
				for c := n.FirstChild; c != nil; c = c.NextSibling {
					if c.Type == html.TextNode {
						title = strings.TrimSpace(c.Data)
						foundTitle = true
						break
					}
				}
			} else if !foundDesc && n.Data == "meta" {
				// Check for description meta tag
				var name, content string
				for _, attr := range n.Attr {
					if strings.EqualFold(attr.Key, "name") {
						name = attr.Val
					} else if strings.EqualFold(attr.Key, "content") {
						content = attr.Val
					}
				}
				if strings.EqualFold(name, "description") {
					description = strings.TrimSpace(content)
					foundDesc = true
				}
			}
		}

		// Continue traversing
		for c := n.FirstChild; c != nil; c = c.NextSibling {
			findContent(c)
		}
	}

	findContent(doc)
	return title, description
}

func main() {
	e := echo.New()
	e.POST("/preview", FetchURLPreview)
	e.Logger.Fatal(e.Start(":8080"))
}
```

   2. We traverse the HTML tree to find:
        - The text inside the <title> tag for the title.

```go
package main

import (
	"bytes"
	"io"
	"net/http"
	"time"

	"github.com/labstack/echo/v4"
	"golang.org/x/net/html"
)

// Request body
type PreviewRequest struct {
	URL string `json:"url"`
}

// Response body
type PreviewResponse struct {
	Title       string `json:"title,omitempty"`
	Description string `json:"description,omitempty"`
}

func FetchURLPreview(ctx echo.Context) error {
	req := new(PreviewRequest)
	if err := ctx.Bind(req); err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "Invalid JSON"})
	}

	// Validate URL
	if req.URL == "" {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusBadRequest, map[string]string{"error": "URL is required"})
	}

	// Fetch the URL
	client := &http.Client{Timeout: 3 * time.Second}
	resp, err := client.Get(req.URL)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": "Failed to fetch URL"})
	}
	defer resp.Body.Close()

	if resp.StatusCode != http.StatusOK {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": "Failed to fetch URL"})
	}

	body, err := io.ReadAll(resp.Body)
	if err != nil {
		return ctx.JSON(http.StatusInternalServerError, map[string]string{"error": "Failed to read response body"})
	}

	title, description := extractTitleAndDescription(body)

	return ctx.JSON(http.StatusOK, PreviewResponse{
		Title:       title,
		Description: description,
	})
}

func extractTitleAndDescription(htmlContent []byte) (string, string) {
	doc, err := html.Parse(bytes.NewReader(htmlContent))
	if err != nil {
		return "", ""
	}

	var title string
	var description string

	var crawler func(*html.Node)
	crawler = func(n *html.Node) {
		if title != "" && description != "" {
			return
		}
		if n.Type == html.ElementNode {
			if n.Data == "title" {
				title = extractText(n)
			} else if n.Data == "meta" {
				var hasDescription bool
				var content string
				for _, attr := range n.Attr {
					if attr.Key == "name" && attr.Val == "description" {
						hasDescription = true
					}
					if attr.Key == "content" {
						content = attr.Val
					}
				}
				if hasDescription && content != "" {
					description = content
				}
			}
		}
		for c := n.FirstChild; c != nil; c = c.NextSibling {
			crawler(c)
		}
	}

	crawler(doc)
	return title, description
}

func extractText(n *html.Node) string {
	if n.Type == html.TextNode {
		return n.Data
	}
	var text string
	for c := n.FirstChild; c != nil; c = c.NextSibling {
		text += extractText(c)
	}
	return text
}

func main() {
	e := echo.New()
	e.POST("/preview", FetchURLPreview)
	e.Start(":8080")
}
```
